message:
fix(abi): fix startup fallback, fork safety, memstream sync, and raw memory ops

startup_abi: When host __libc_start_main delegation fails via dlvsym,
fall back to calling main() directly with init/fini instead of returning
-1 with ENOSYS. Add explicit comment explaining why RUNTIME_READY is
intentionally NOT set (membrane re-entrancy deadlocks).

stdio_abi: Add fork safety to popen() — call run_atfork_prepare(),
acquire pipeline lock, then run_atfork_child/parent after clone. Fix
open_memstream sync_memstream_to_caller to use realloc() instead of
malloc() to prevent leaking the previous buffer on each flush.
Refactor MemStreamSync to an enum with Dynamic (open_memstream) and
Fixed (fmemopen) variants so fmemopen streams also sync their buffer
content on flush/close.

string_abi: Revert std::ptr::copy_nonoverlapping back to byte-by-byte
volatile ops for raw_memcpy/memmove/memset — the compiler was
optimizing std::ptr calls into calls to our interposed memcpy/memmove
symbols, causing infinite recursion. Volatile ops prevent this.

Also expand wchar_abi, unistd_abi, process_abi, and stdlib_abi with
additional interposed function coverage. Update CVE arena test results.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
